observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that:
1.10.1 The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the different offices on the
CY 2024 year-end balances of inventories and prepare the adjusting
entries, so as to correct the balances of the inventory and equity
accounts in the ensuing financial statements;
1.10.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories
and to prepare and maintain the forms (AIRs, RIS and SSMI), and
reports (stock and ledger cards) for proper accounting and control of
the acquisition and issuance, thereof; and
1.10.3 The Inventory Committee conduct semestral physical count of
inventories and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI), and submit of copies thereof to the accounting office and the
Audit Team.

2.8 We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Disposal and
Appraisal Committee:
2.8.1 Conduct evaluation of construction and heavy equipment and motor
vehicles to determine items for disposal, by way of public auction or
applicable mode of disposal, in accordance with the guidelines set
under COA-DBM Joint Circular 2024-1; and
2.8.2 Assess the other identified unserviceable and destroyed property
items, and initiate disposal procedures to prevent further physical
degradation.

3.13 We recommended that Management:
3.13.1 Rescind existing contracts for ongoing projects that have surpassed
10 per cent of the contract time without corresponding work
accomplishment, as required by the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
3.13.2 Enforce timely project execution by strengthening internal controls and
require contractors and administrative teams to follow approved work
schedules and target dates in the implementation of infrastructure
projects;
3.13.3 Promptly notify contractors upon expiration of contract durations prior
to the imposition of liquidated damages, and evaluate the possibility of
contract rescissions where delays exceed 10 per cent of contract times;
3.13.4 Evaluate contractor performances, particularly with regard to
adherence to project timelines;
3.13.5 Establish clear target dates and implementation schedules of projects
undertaken by administration, and regularly monitor progress to
facilitate their timely and efficient completion; and
3.13.6 Improve monitoring systems through the project monitoring team to
oversee both contracted and in-house projects and provide regular
updates to the LCE.

4.7 We recommended and Management agreed to:
4.7.1 Submit a complete list of all government projects/programs/activities,
completed and on-going, and those to be implemented in CY 2025; and
thereafter, submit accomplishment reports to the Audit Team at the end
of each quarter;
4.7.2 Regularly update and post project signboards at project sites,
indicating current status of work accomplished based on the
prescribed schedule; and
4.7.3 Inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of any
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the
appropriate project signboards and/or public notices have already been
posted, to enable proper validation.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
5.6.1 Review and assess all completed infrastructure projects to determine
the appropriate asset classification in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2015-009;
5.6.2 Identify which of the assets should be transferred to the barangays, and
prepare the necessary turnover documents; and
5.6.3 Record or reclassify the assets to their proper PPE accounts to ensure
accuracy, consistency and reliability of the financial statements, and
clarity among financial statement users.

6.5 We recommended and Management, through the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council, agreed to:
6.5.1 Ensure that only PPAs aligned with the DRRM thematic areas - Disaster
Prevention and Mitigation, Preparedness, Response and Rehabilitation
and Recovery - are included in the LDRRMFIP, in accordance with DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and
6.5.2 Charge all expenditures related to PS, MOOE and CO of the LDRRMO
to the General Fund, pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01.

7.8 We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer ensure the cash transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-
QRF and MOOE to the TF books of accounts at yearend.

8.13 We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
8.13.1 Locate and submit the necessary budget documents supporting the
₱8,000,000.00 employee economic loan program, ensuring that no
appropriated funds were diverted from their intended public use; and
8.13.2 Stop further disbursements for economic loans to employees from the
General Fund, and ensure that public funds are used strictly for public
purposes, in compliance with Sections 305 and 447 of the Local
Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a complete
and updated certified list showing the names of taxpayers with the amount of
taxes due and collectible based on the RPT Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index
Cards at the beginning of the year. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant to then
use this list as basis in setting-up the receivables at the beginning of the year; and

b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile records with the Municipal Treasurer, and
make the necessary adjustments, where appropriate, to come-up with the
correct, reconciled and fairly present the balances of RPT and SET
Receivables in the SFPos.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the affected
accounts in the SFPos. Thereafter, recognize the liabilities relating to salaries,
benefits and other emoluments of the officers and employees using the Due to
Officers and Employees as prescribed under the RCA for LGUs, Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year and submit the
same to the Municipal Accountant who, on the basis thereof, shall prepare the JEV
for the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of
the appropriate payable/liability accounts at year-end and in addition, reverse the
entries made in the ensuing year to fairly present the balances of the affected
accounts in the Statement of Financial Position (SFPos).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the
necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the
SFPer. Thereafter, recognize the municipal aids to component Barangays using the
Subsidy to Other LGUs as prescribed under the RCA for LGUs, Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
BAC to submit to the COA Audit Team for evaluation all the lacking documents
relative to the subject procurements, otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in
audit. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all payments to
suppliers and contractors are supported with complete documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and other concerned
officials to stop the practice of splitting contracts and instead, consolidate all
requests for the procurement of related purchases and pursue competitive bidding in
the procurement thereof, unless resort to other alternative mode of procurement is
warranted by the conditions set forth by the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Local Finance Committee, Municipal Accountant and other responsible
officials to submit a specific, measurable, attainable, relevant, and time-bound
action plan that will outline their recommended fiscal policies and strategies to
address the problem of SEF unrealized income that the Municipality had been
experiencing over the years; and

b. Local Finance Committee to strictly set a more realistic budget based on
previous year’s actual performance in terms of revenues and expenses in
accordance with the existing guidelines on budget preparation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and
GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson to ensure that GAD Fund are utilized
solely for Programs, Projects and Activities (PPA) that is responsive to the identified
gender issues, concerns and GAD mandates pursuant to the provisions of PCW, NEDA
and DBM JC No. 2012-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the concerned official/s to exert prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and immediately stop the charging of monthly wages of municipal job hires against the LDRRMF; 
b. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to strictly utilize the LDRRMF for the projects/activities enumerated in Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspensions or Disallowances; and

observations.reason: Concerned officials did not provide explanation for the non-implementa-tion of the recommen-dation.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended Management to:
a. impose the immediate liquidation of Cash Advances;
b. avoid granting additional cash advance until the previous cash advances have
been liquidated;
c. in cases of excessive cash advance, require the Accountable Officer to return or
refund immediately any excess thereof; and
d. monitor the timely liquidation of cash advances by sending reminders to
accountable officers.

3.5 We recommended Management to:
a. utilize fully idle or dormant trust funds pursuant to the law governing said
grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the respective
donors;
b. implement proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund
of excess project funds to the donor, if so provided in the terms of the grant.

4.5 We recommended Management to:
a. submit all lacking supporting documents enumerated in Annex D; and
b. require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal
 Accountant to strictly comply with the documentary requirements
stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

5.6 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance to the special trust fund in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

6.7 We recommended Management to prioritize the identification of a Municipal
controlled dumpsite and establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in every
barangay.

7.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Local School Board
to ensure that the Special Education Fund is budgeted and fully utilized for the
purpose for which it was created which is to promote quality education in
every public school.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  concerned
   officials to submit to the COA  Audit  Team  supplemental  Accomplishment
   Reports showing in  details  the  assistance  provided  by  the  five  JO
   personnel to disaster preparedness and/or response, for evaluation of the
   Audit Team. Otherwise, we recommend that the total amount of  P292,250.00
   or part thereof corresponding to days or months  they  did  not  work  on
   disaster management be reverted to the LDRRMF.

                  Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct
   concerned officials to stop the charging of wages against the  LDRRMF  of
   Job Order Personnel whose works are not clearly and directly  related  to
   disaster risk reduction and management

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  direct  the  OIC-  MDRRMO  to
   coordinate with the DILG  and  NDRRMC  and  other  concerned  offices  to
   determine the availability and schedules of  Resource  Persons  in  their
   pool and get their commitments within reasonable time to ensure that  the
   trainings and seminars  are  conducted  as  planned  or  adjusted  timely
   according to availability of the Resource Persons.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the BAC,
   OIC-GSO and other concerned officials to  consolidate  all  requests  for
   similar supplies, equipment and other items in the APP to avoid splitting
   of purchases and pursue competitive bidding in the  procurement  thereof,
   unless resort to shopping or other alternative  mode  of  procurement  is
   warranted by the conditions set forth by the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  direct  the  OIC-MENRO  to
   propose to the Sangguniang Bayan  the  inclusion  in  the  Municipality’s
   budget funds to support the closure of the open  dumpsite,  establishment
   of the MRF, the creation of a SWMB and the  preparation  of  the  10-year
   SWMP as required under RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  and  the  Municipal  Vice
   Mayor submit to the COA Audit Team supporting documents for  the  subject
   meal expenses, particularly  showing  the  purposes  of  the  travels  or
   meetings/visits, the names of the guests and government agencies visited,
   the  projects  involved  and  justification  that  the  expenditures  are
   necessary in the performance of their duties and in the attainment of the
   objectives or thrusts of the Municipality,  to  avoid  suspension  and/or
   disallowance in audit.


        Thereafter, we recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  institutes
   measures or policies enjoining all Municipality’s officials and employees
   to:


        a. observe austerity and economy by providing  guests  with  simple
           meals or snacks;
        b. conduct meetings with constituents inside the Municipal Hall;
        c. ensure  that  all  supporting  documents  showing  the  official
           purpose of  the  activities/meetings,  names  of  guests  to  be
           entertained and other relevant information and justification for
           entertaining them outside of  the  Municipality’s  premises  are
           attached to the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


        a) direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Bureau of
           Internal Revenue and the Cavite Provincial Treasurer’s Office to
           determine the  amount  of  taxes  due  and  the  possibility  or
           legalities of entering into compromise agreement as  to  payment
           of penalties and surcharges;


        b) investigate if there are officials who  had  been  negligent  in
           ensuring that the  relevant  Deeds  of  Sale  provided  for  the
           payments of taxes and other expenses and to include them in  the
           budget or to deduct them from the gross payments to the sellers,
           and take appropriate action, including demanding that  taxes  be
           personally shouldered by them.


        Henceforth, we recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the
   Municipal Accountant to ensure that agreements on payments of  taxes  and
   expenses related to procurement of lands are clearly provided for in  the
   Deeds of Sale and that all documentary requirements for  the  titling  of
   lots are promptly processed and submitted to the Register of Deeds.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Bids  and
   Awards Committee and other concerned officials to study the legality  and
   propriety of rebidding the Salintubig  project  at  the  Approved  Budget
   Contract (ABC) of P400,000.00. If this is not  feasible,  we  recommended
   that  he  direct  concerned  officials  to  study  the   possibility   of
   appropriating  in  the  next  budget  year  counterpart  funds  from  the
   Municipality or to coordinate with  the  DOH  to  determine  whether  the
   Municipality could still request for additional funds  for  the  project.
   Otherwise, we recommended that the amount of P400,000.00 be  returned  to
   the DOH.
